Package neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.phaseenvelopeops.multicomponentenvelopeops

Class EnvelopeSegment

java.lang.Object
neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.phaseenvelopeops.multicomponentenvelopeops.EnvelopeSegment
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class EnvelopeSegment extends Object implements Serializable
One contiguous branch of a PT phase envelope.

Modern envelope tracers emit the envelope as a list of segments instead of flat arrays. Each segment represents a polyline with a known phase type (dew or bubble) so downstream code (plotting, flash initialization, critical-point analysis) does not have to guess where one branch ends and another begins.

Segments are produced by splitting the internal per-point lists on NaN sentinel values that the tracer inserts at every branch transition (primary-to-restart pass and critical-point crossings). A segment always contains ≥ 1 point and never contains NaN.

Version:
1.0
Author:
asmund

  • Constructor Details

    • EnvelopeSegment

      public EnvelopeSegment(EnvelopeSegment.PhaseType phaseType, double[] temperatures, double[] pressures, double[] enthalpies, double[] densities, double[] entropies)
      Construct an immutable segment.
      Parameters:
      phaseType - dew or bubble
      temperatures - temperatures in Kelvin (length ≥ 1, no NaN)
      pressures - pressures in bara (same length as temperatures)
      enthalpies - enthalpies in kJ/kg (same length, may be all-zero if not tracked)
      densities - densities in kg/m3 (same length, may be all-zero if not tracked)
      entropies - entropies in kJ/kg/K (same length, may be all-zero if not tracked)

  • Method Details

    • getPhaseType

      public EnvelopeSegment.PhaseType getPhaseType()
      Segment phase type.
      Returns:
      DEW or BUBBLE

    • size

      public int size()
      Number of points in this segment.
      Returns:
      length of the arrays

    • getTemperatures

      public double[] getTemperatures()
      Temperatures along this segment.
      Returns:
      defensive copy of the temperature array, in Kelvin

    • getPressures

      public double[] getPressures()
      Pressures along this segment.
      Returns:
      defensive copy of the pressure array, in bara

    • getEnthalpies

      public double[] getEnthalpies()
      Mass enthalpies along this segment.
      Returns:
      defensive copy of the enthalpy array, in kJ/kg

    • getDensities

      public double[] getDensities()
      Mass densities along this segment.
      Returns:
      defensive copy of the density array, in kg/m3

      public double[] getEntropies()
      Mass entropies along this segment.
      Returns:
      defensive copy of the entropy array, in kJ/kg/K
